verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “How then shall they call on Him in Whom they have not believed? and how shall they believe in Him of Whom they have not heard? and how shall they hear without a preacher? and how shall they preach except they be sent? as it is written.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here again he takes from them all excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since he had said, “I bear them record that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge,” and that “being ignorant of God’s righteousness, they submitted not themselves” to it: he next shows, that for this ignorance itself they were punishable before God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This he does not say indeed so, but he makes it good by carrying on his discourse in the way of question, and so convicting them more clearly, by framing the whole passage out of objections and answers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But look further back.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Prophet, saith he, said, “Whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord shall be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now somebody might say perhaps, “But how could they call upon Him Whom they had not believed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then there is a question from him after the objection; And why did they not believe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then an objection again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: A person certainly may say, And how could they believe, since they had not heard?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet hear they did, he implies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then another objection again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And how could they hear without a preacher?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then an answer again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet preach they did, and there were many sent forth for this very purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And whence does it appear that these are those persons sent?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then he brings the prophet in next, who says, “How beautiful are the feet of them that preach the Gospel of peace, and bring glad tidings of good things!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see how by the kind of preaching he points out the preachers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there was nothing else that these men went about telling everywhere, but those unspeakable good things, and the peace made by God with men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so by disbelieving, it is not we, he implies, whom you disbelieve, but Isaiah the prophet, who spake many years ago, that we were to be sent, and to preach, and to say what we do say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If the being saved, then, came of calling upon Him, and calling upon Him from believing, and believing from hearing, and hearing from preaching, and preaching from being sent, and if they were sent, and did preach, and the prophet went round with them to point them out, and proclaim them, and say that these were they whom they showed of so many ages ago, whose feet even they praised because of the matter of their preaching; then it is quite clear that the not believing was their own fault only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that because God’s part had been fulfilled completely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But they have not all obeyed the Gospel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath believed our report?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.” (ib. liii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since they pressed him with another objection again to this effect, that if these were the persons sent upon the mission by God, all ought to have hearkened to them: observe Paul’s judgment, and see how he shows that this very thing which made the confusion, did in fact do away with confusion and embarrassment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What offends you, O Jew, he would say, after so great and abundant evidence, and demonstration of the points? that all did not submit to the Gospel?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why this very thing, when taken along with the others, is of force to certify thee of the truth of my statements, even in that some do not believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this too the prophet foretold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Notice his unspeakable wisdom too; how he shows more than they were looking for, or expected him to have to say in reply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what is it that you say? he means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is it that all have not believed the Gospel?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Isaiah foretold this too from of old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather, not this only, but even much more than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the complaint you make is Why did not all believe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But Isaiah goes further than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what is it he says?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Lord, who hath believed our report?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then since he had rid himself of this embarrassment. by making the Prophet a bulwark against them, he again keeps to the line he was before upon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as he had said that they must call upon Him, but that they who call must believe, and they who believe must hear first, but they who are to hear must have preachers, and the preachers be sent, and as he had shown that they were sent, and had preached; as he is going to bring in another objection again, taking occasion first of another quotation from the Prophet, by which he had met the objection a little back, he thus interweaves it, and connects it with what went before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since he had produced the Prophet as saying, “Lord, who hath believed our report” (ἀκοᾕ)? he happily seizes on the quotation, as proving what he says, “So then faith cometh by hearing” (ἀκοἥς).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he makes not a mere naked statement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But as the Jews were forever seeking a sign, and the sight of the Resurrection, and were gaping after the thing much; he says, Yet the Prophet promised no such thing, but that it was by hearing that we were to believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he makes this good first, and says, “so then faith cometh by hearing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then since this seemed a mean thing to say, see how he elevates it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he says, I was not speaking of mere hearing, nor of the need of hearing men’s words and believing them, but I mean a great sort of hearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the hearing is “by the word of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They were not speaking their own, but they were telling what they learnt from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is a higher thing than miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For we are equally bound to believe and to obey God, whether speaking or working miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since both works and miracles come of His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For both the heaven and everything else was established in this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Ps. xxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After showing then that we ought to believe the prophets, who always speak God’s words, and not to look after anything more, he proceeds next to the objection I mentioned, and says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But I say, Have they not heard?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What, he means, if the preachers were sent, and did preach what they were bid, and these did not hear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then comes a most perfect reply to the objection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Yes, verily, their sound went into all the earth, and their words unto the ends of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What do you say? he means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They have not heard?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why the whole world, and the ends of the earth, have heard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And have you, amongst whom the heralds abode such a long time, and of whose land they were, not heard?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now can this ever be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Sure if the ends of the world heard, much more must you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then again another objection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But I say, Did not Israel know?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what if they heard, he means, but did not know what was said, nor understand that these were the persons sent?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Are they not to be forgiven for their ignorance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For Isaiah had described their character in the words, “How beautiful are the feet of them that preach the Gospel of peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And before him the Lawgiver himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence he proceeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “First Moses saith, I will provoke you to jealousy by them that are no people, and by a foolish nation I will anger you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Deut. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so they ought even from him to have been able to distinguish the preachers, not from the fact of these disbelieving only, not from the fact of their preaching peace, not from the fact of their bringing the glad tidings of those good things, not from the word being sown in every part of the world, but from the very fact of their seeing their inferiors, those of the Gentiles, in greater honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what they had never heard, nor their forefathers, that wisdom did these on a sudden embrace (ἑφιλοσόφουν).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this was a mark of such intense honor, as should gall them, and lead them to jealousy, and to recollection of the prophecy of Moses, which said, “I will provoke you to jealousy by them that are no people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was not the greatness of the honor alone that was enough to throw them upon jealousy, but the fact too that a nation had come to enjoy these things which was of so little account that it could hardly be considered a nation at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For I will provoke you to jealousy, by them which are no nation, and by a foolish nation will I anger you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what more foolish than the Greeks (Heathen, see pp.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 373, 377)? or what of less account?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how by every means God had given from of old indications and clear signs of these times, in order to remove their blindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was not any little corner in which the thing was done, but in land, and in sea, and in every quarter of the globe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And they saw those in the enjoyment of countless blessings now, who had formerly been objects of their contempt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: One should consider then that this is that people of which Moses said, “I will provoke you to jealousy by them that are no people, and by a foolish nation will I anger you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Was it Moses only then that said this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: No, for Isaiah also after Him saith so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is why Paul said, “First Moses,” to show that a second will come who says the same things in a clearer and plainer way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then he says above, that Esaias crieth, so too here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But Esaias is very bold, and saith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what he means is something of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He put a violence on himself, and was ambitious to speak, not some thing veiled over, but to set things even naked before your eyes, and choosing rather to run (Origen in loc.) into dangers from being plain spoken, than by looking to his own safety, to leave you any shelter for your impenetrableness; although it was not the manner of prophecy to say this so clearly; but still to stop your mouths most completely, he tells the whole beforehand clearly and distinctly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The whole! what whole?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why your being cast out, and also their being brought in; speaking as follows, “I was found of them that sought Me not, I was made manifest of them that asked not after Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who then are they that sought not? who they that asked not after Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Clearly not the Jews, but they of the Gentiles, who hitherto had not known Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then Moses gave their characteristic mark in the words, “no people” and “a foolish nation,” so here also he takes the same ground to point them out from, viz. their extreme ignorance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this was a very great blame to attach to the Jews, that they who sought Him not found Him, and they who sought Him lost Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But unto Israel He saith, All the day long have I stretched forth My hands unto a disobedient and gainsaying people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Observe now that difficulty, which so many make a subject of question, is discovered laid up from of old in the words of the Prophet, and with a clear solution to it too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You heard Paul say before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “What shall we say then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That the Gentiles which followed not after righteousness have attained unto righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But Israel which followed after the law of righteousness hath not attained to the law of righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This Esaias also says here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to say, “I was found of them that sought me not, I was made manifest unto them which asked not after me,” is the same with saying, “that the Gentiles which followed not after righteousness have attained unto righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to show that what was happening was not of God’s grace only, but also of the temper of those who came to Him, as also the casting off of the others came of the disputatiousness of those who disobeyed, hear what he proceeds with.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But to Israel He saith, All the day long have I stretched forth My hands to a disobedient and gainsaying people;” here meaning by the day the whole period of the former dispensation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the stretching out of the hands, means calling and drawing them to Him, and inviting them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to show that the fault was all their own, he says “to a disobedient and gainsaying people.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see what a great charge this is against them!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they did not obey Him even when He invited them, but they gainsaid Him, and that when they saw Him doing so, not once or twice or thrice, but the whole period.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But others who had never known Him, had the power to draw Him to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Not that he says they themselves had the power to do it, but to take away lofty imaginings even from those of the Gentiles, and to show that it was His grace that wrought the whole, He says, I was made manifest, and I was found.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It may be said, Were they then void of everything?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By no means, for the taking of the things found, and the getting a knowledge of what was manifested to them, was what they contributed themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to prevent these saying, But why wast Thou not made manifest to us also? he sets down what is more than this, that I not only was made manifest, but I even continue with My hands stretched out, inviting them, and displaying all the concern of an affectionate father, and a mother that is set on her child.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how he has brought us a most lucid answer to all the difficulties before raised, by showing that it was from their own temper that ruin had befallen them, and that they are wholly undeserving of pardon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For though they had both heard and understood what was said, still not even then were they minded to come to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is far more, He did not cause them to hear these things and to understand them only, but a thing which hath more force to rouse them up and draw them to Him, when they were disobedient and gain-saying, He added to the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is His exasperating them, and making them jealous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For ye know the domineering might of the passion, and how great the power is which jealousy is naturally possessed of for bringing all disputatiousness to an end, and rousing those who have grown remiss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why need one say this of man when in brutes without reason, and children before they are of full age, the power it shows is so great?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For a child often will not submit to its father when it is called, but continues obstinate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when another child has notice taken of it, then it even though not called comes to its father’s bosom, and what calling could not do, provoking to jealousy will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then God also did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For He not only called and stretched out His hands, but stirred up in them the feeling of jealousy also, by bringing those far inferior to them (a thing which makes men excessively jealous) not into their good things, but (what was a much stronger step, and makes the feeling even more domineering,) into much greater good things, and of greater necessity than theirs, and such as they had never even fancied in a dream.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But still they did not submit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What pardon then do they deserve who exhibit such excessive obstinacy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet this he does not say himself, but leaves it to the consciences of his hearers, to gather it from the conclusion of what he had stated, and again also confirms it by what he goes on to in his usual wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he did also above, by introducing objections both in the case of the Law (see on Rom. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 420, I) and of the people, which presented an accusation beyond the true one; and then in the answer, which was to overthrow this, yielding as much as he pleased, and as the case allowed, so as to make what he was saying not unwelcome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he doth here, writing as follows:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Chap. xi. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “I say then, Hath God cast away His people whom He foreknew?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: God forbid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he introduces the form a person would use in doubt, as though taking occasion from what had been said, and after making this alarming statement, by the denial of it he causes the sequel to be allowed with readiness; and what by all the former arguments he had been laboring to show that he makes good here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

verses.text: What could be more brutal cruelty than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then could they have to say to this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

verses.text: But they distressed you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

verses.text: For you cannot, he means, so much as say, that the Prophets called indeed, and God invited, and the state of things cried aloud, and the provoking to jealousy was enough to draw us to Him, but what was enjoined was grievous, and this is why we could not draw nigh, since we had a display of works demanded of us, and laborious well-doings.

verses.text: For the former insolence did not show us so ungrateful as that committed after honor and much attention shown us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Are they unnumbered, the fearful and outrageous acts done by thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what of this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For hitherto thou art not gone away into the grave where no man shah confess. (ib. xxxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hitherto the arena (θέατρον) is not broken up for thee, but thou art standing within the line, and thou art able even by a struggle at the last to recover all thy defeats.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thou art not yet come to where the rich man was, for thee to hear it said, “there is a gulf betwixt you and us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Bridegroom is not yet at hand, that one should fear to give you of his oil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still canst thou buy and store up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And there is not one yet to say, “Not so; lest there be not enough for us and and you” (Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 9); but there are many that sell, the naked, the hungry, the sick, the imprisoned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Give food to these, clothing to those, visit the sick, and the oil will come more than from fountains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The day of account is not here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Use the time as need be, and make deductions from the debts, and to him that oweth “an hundred measures of oil, say, Take thy bill and write fifty.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with money, and with words, and with every other thing do in like manner, imitating that steward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And advise this to thyself, and also to thy relatives, for thou hast still the power of saying so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thou art not yet come to the necessity of calling in another in their behalf, but thou hast power to give advice at once to thyself and to others. (ib.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when thou art gone away thither, neither of these things wilt thou have it in thy power to do at need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with good reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For thou who hast had so long a period fixed thee, and neither done thyself good, nor any else, how when thou art under the Judge’s hands shalt thou be able to obtain this grace?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Putting all these things together then, let us cling fast to our own salvation, and not lose the opportunity of this life present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is possible, it is, even at our last breath to please God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is possible to gain approval by thy last will, not indeed in such way as in our lifetime, still it is possible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How, and in what way?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If thou leavest Him among thine heirs, and givest Him also (καὶ αὐτᾥ) a portion of thine whole estate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hast thou not fed Him in thy lifetime?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: At all events when departed, when thou art no longer owner, give Him a share of thy goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He is loving unto man, He doth not deal niggardly by thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is a mark to be sure of a greater desire, and so it will be more rewarded, to feed Him in thy lifetime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou hast not done this, at all events do the next best thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Leave Him joint-heir (see p.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 384) with thy children, and if thou art dilatory over this, bethink thyself that His Father made thee joint-heir with Him, and break down thy inhuman spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what excuse wilt thou have if thou dost not even make Him a sharer with thy children, who made thee share the Heaven, and was slain for thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet all that ever He did, He did not in repayment of a debt, but as bestowing a favor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But you after so great benefits, have been made a debtor as well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet, though things are so, it is as if receiving a favor, not as demanding payment of a debt, that He crowneth thee; and this too when what He is to receive is His own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Give then thy money, which is now no longer of any use to thee, and of which thou art no longer owner; and He will give thee a Kingdom which shall be of service to thee perpetually, and with it will bestow also the things of this life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if He be made the joint heir of thy children, He doth lighten their orphanage for them, do away with plots against them, beat off insults, stop the mouths of pettifoggers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if they themselves be unable to stand up for their bequeathments, He will Himself stand up, and not let them be broken through.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if He do even allow this, then He makes up of Himself all that was ordered in the will with still greater liberality, because He has been but mentioned in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Leave Him then thine heir.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is to Him that thou art upon the point of going.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He will be thy Judge Himself in the trial for all that hath been done here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But there are some so miserable and pinched, that though they have no children, still they have not the courage to do this, but approve of giving that they have to hangers on, and to flatterers, and to this person and to that, sooner than to Christ, Who hath done them so great benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what can be more unreasonable than this conduct?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if one were to compare men of this cast to asses, aye, or to stones, one shall not still be saying anything tantamount to their unreasonableness and senselessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Nor could one find a similitude to put before you their madness and dementedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what pardon shall they obtain for not having fed Him in their lifetime, who, even when they are on the point of departing to Him, have not the inclination to give Him but a trifle out of those goods, of which they are no longer the owners, but are of such an inimical and hostile disposition, as not even to give Him a share in what is useless to themselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do you not know how many of mankind have not even been counted worthy to obtain an end of this kind, but have been snatched off suddenly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But thee doth God empower to give orders to thy kindred, and to speak with them about thy property, and set all that is in thy house in order.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What defence then wilt thou have to set up, when even after receiving this favor from Him, thou hast treacherously given up the benefit, and art standing as it were in diametrical opposition to thy forefathers in the faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they even in their lifetime sold all, and brought it to the Apostle’s feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But thou, even at thy death, dost not give any share to them that need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is the better part, and gives one much boldness, is to remedy poverty in one’s lifetime.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou hast not been minded to do this, at all events do upon thy death-bed some noble act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is not what a strong love for Christ would do, yet still it is an act of love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if thou wilt not have the high place with the Lambs, still even to be after them at all is no light thing, and so not to be placed with the goats nor on the left hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou wilt not do even this, what plea is to rescue thee, when neither the fear of death nor thy money having become henceforth of no use to thee, nor the leaving of safety behind thee to thy children, nor the laying up of much pardon there against the time to come, will make thee merciful to man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore I advise, as the best thing, that in your lifetime you give the larger half of your goods to the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if there be any of so narrow a soul as not to have the heart to do so, at all events let them by necessity become merciful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when you were living as if there were no death, then you clung close to your goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But now since you have learnt that you are to die, at least now give over your opinion, and deliberate about your affairs as one that must die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather as one that ought to enjoy immortal life for evermore.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if what I am going to say be distasteful, and big with horror, still it must be said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Reckon with thy slaves the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Art thou giving thy slaves liberty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Give Christ liberty from famine, from distress, from imprisonment, from nakedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Art thou horrified at the words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is it not then more horrible when thou dost not even thus much?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And here the word makes thy blood curdle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when thou art gone to that world, and hast to hear things far more grievous than these, and seest the tortures which are incurable, what wilt thou say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To whom wilt thou flee for refuge?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whom wilt thou call to thy alliance and assistance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Will it be Abraham?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He will not hearken to thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or those virgins?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They will not give thee of their oil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thy father then or thy grandfather?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But none even of these, if he be ever so holy, will have it in his power to reverse that sentence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Weighing then all these things, to Him Who alone is Lord to blot out the bill against thee and to quench that flame, to Him make prayer and supplication, and propitiate Him, by now feeding Him and clothing Him continually: that in this world thou mayest depart with a good hope, and when thou art there thou mayest enjoy eternal blessings, which may we all attain to by the grace and love toward man, etc.
